titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Toshiyuki Takamiya Collection of Arthur Charles Fox-Davies
Takamiya (Toshiyuki) Collection of Arthur Charles Fox-Davies
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 28, 2024 at 00:06

did.unittitle: Toshiyuki Takamiya collection of Arthur Charles Fox-Davies

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, notebooks, printed material, notes, a bookplate collection, and other papers relating to heraldry and Arthur Fox-Davies.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Maruzen-Yushodo Company, Ltd. on the Hazel M. Osborn Fund, 2020.

bioghist.head: Toshiyuki Takamiya (1944-)
bioghist.p: Toshiyuki Takamiya (高宮利行) is a Japanese author and scholar of medieval English literature. He is a professor of English at Keio University in Tokyo and also a collector of antiquarian books.

bioghist.head: Arthur Charles Fox-Davies (1871-1928)
bioghist.p: Arthur Charles Fox-Davies was a British barrister and expert on heraldry most known for The Complete Guide to Heraldry , published in 1909. Other works included The Art of Heraldry: An Encyclopædia of Armory (1904). Fox-Davies also served as editor of Dod's Peerage , Burke's Landed Gentry , and Genealogical Magazine . In 1901, he married Mary Ellen Blanche Crookes, who worked as a heraldic artist, often for her husband's publications, under the pseudonym C. Helard.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Toshiyuki Takamiya Collection of Arthur Charles Fox-Davies. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, notebooks, printed material, notes, a bookplate collection, and other papers relating to heraldry and Arthur Fox-Davies. Included are drawings by Mary Ellen Blanche Crookes, known professionally as C. Helard, G. W. Eve, Graham Johnston, and George Scruby.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Toshiyuki Takamiya Collection of Arthur Charles Fox-Davies is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

persname.part: Crookes, M. E. B. (Mary Ellen Blanche), 1870-1935

did.unittitle: Heraldic drawings by G. W. Eve and others
